title: uint2uf
html:
uint2uf

  • Last UpdatedOkt 13, 2024
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Copy the specified integer value to a basic parameter or entity field.

long uint2uf (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, int intVal);

Parameters

hOpr
The handle of an operation or entity
seqNr
The sequence number of the parameter or field (sequence numbers start with 1)
intVal
The integer value to be copied to the parameter or field

Return Values

For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic.

Use

Library: ucall

Description

uint2uf copies the supplied integer value to a basic parameter or entity field.

During this copy, the data is converted from external format to Uniface format. If you have data that is already in Uniface format, use uprmputs rather than uint2uf.

Example: uentoccs, uint2uf, uuf2int

The following extended example demonstrates the use of uentoccs, and other entity- and parameter-related functions.

The entity CUSTOMER is defined as:

CUSTNUM integer
NAME char(20)
ADDRESS char(20)
ACCOUNTNUM integer

The service CUSTSRV contains two operations. One is GETCUST which has CUSTOMER as an OUT parameter. This operation creates 3 occurrences of CUSTOMER. The other operation is SETCUST which has CUSTOMER as an IN parameter. It prints all occurrences to the message frame.

Operation GETCUST and SETCUST

The operations GETCUST and SETCUST in service CUSTSRV are defined as follows:

operation getcust
params
entity customer : out;
endparams

creocc "customer", -1
CUSTNUM = 123
name = "john doe"
address = "someplace"
accountnum = 123456

creocc "customer", -1
CUSTNUM = 234
name = "jane doe"
address = "anywhere"
accountnum = 234567

creocc "customer", -1
CUSTNUM = 345
name = "flabber gast"
address = "unknown"
accountnum = 345678

return 0
end

operation setcust
params
entity customer : in;
endparams

setocc "customer", 1
putmess "%%custnum %%name %%address %%accountnum"
setocc "customer", 2
putmess "%%custnum %%name %%address %%accountnum"
setocc "customer", 3
putmess "%%custnum %%name %%address %%accountnum"
return 0
end
3GL code

Our 3GL code first activates operation SETCUST to get the entity data. It passes on this data to SETCUST, but with all STRING fields converted to uppercase. To do this, we loop through all the occurrences and fields of our output entity parameter, creating occurrences and filling fields of the input entity parameter as necessary. Finally we call SETCUST that prints the converted data to the message frame:

123 JOHN DOE SOMEPLACE 123456
234 JANE DOE ANYWHERE 234567
345 FLABBER GAST UNKNOWN 345678

Note that the 3GL code treats every field that is not a STRING as an INTEGER. This happens to be sufficient for this particular entity. A more generic approach would probably use uprmgets and uprmputs to handle all fields not of type STRING.

The 3GL code is as follows:

#include <h3gl.h>
#include <ctype.h>

void convert_customer ()
{
UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */
UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */
long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */
UHACT hPrm; /* Parameter handle */
UHACT hEnt1, hEnt2; /* Entity handles */
long numOccs, numFlds; /* Nr of occurrrences and fields */
long iOcc, iFld; /* Indices of occurrence and field */
int fType; /* Field datatype */
UCHAR* strVal; /* Allocated string buffer */
UCHAR *p; /* Work pointer */
int intVal; /* Integer value */

ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */
/* Instantiate service TESTSVC*/
uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"CUSTSRV", 0, 0,
UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);

/* Instantiate operation GETCUST */
/* Instantiate operation SETCUST */
uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETCUST", 0, &hOp1);
uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETCUST", 0, &hOp2);

uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETCUST */
uprmgeth(hOp1, 1, 0, &hEnt1); /* Get OUT handle for CUSTOMER*/
uprmgeth(hOp2, 1, 0, &hEnt2); /* Get IN handle for CUSTOMER*/

uentoccs (hEnt1, &numOccs); /* Get number of occurrences */
uentflds (hEnt1, &numFlds); /* Get number of fields */

for ( iOcc=1; iOcc<=numOccs; iOcc++ )/* Loop through occurrences */
{
uentseto(hEnt1, iOcc); /* Make occurrence current */
uentcreo(hEnt2, iOcc); /* Create new occ for SETCUST */

for ( iFld=1; iFld<=numFlds; iFld++ )/* Loop through fields */
{
uprmgeth(hEnt1, iFld, 0, &hPrm); /* Get field handle */
uprmtype(hPrm, &fType); /* Get field type */

if ( fType == UTYPE_STRING ) /* Handle string field */
{
uuf2str(hEnt1, iFld, &strVal); /* Get string data */
for ( p=strVal; *p; p++ )
*p = toupper(*p); /* Convert to uppercase */
ustr2uf(hEnt2, iFld, strVal); /* Put converted data */
unifree(hEnt1, strVal); /* Free string buffer */
}
else
{
uuf2int(hEnt1, iFld, &intVal);/* Get integer value */
uint2uf(hEnt2, iFld, intVal); /* Put integer value */
}

ufreeh(&hPrm); /* Free parameter handle */
}
}

uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation SETENT */
uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */
ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free GETCUST operation handle */
ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free SETCUST operation handle */
ufreeh (&hEnt1); /* Free entity handle */
ufreeh (&hEnt2); /* Free entity handle */
ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */
ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */
}
Related Links
  • Call-In API Return Codes

    Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.

  • uentoccs

    Return the number of occurrences in the specified entity parameter.

  • uprmputs

    Fill a basic parameter or entity field with Uniface-formatted data.

  • uuf2int

    Copy an integer value from a basic parameter or entity field to an int program variable.

It passes on this data to SETCUST, but with all STRING fields converted to uppercase. To do this, we loop through all the occurrences and fields of our output entity parameter, creating occurrences and filling fields of the input entity parameter as necessary. Finally we call SETCUST that prints the converted data to the message frame: Explain this code 123 JOHN DOE SOMEPLACE 123456 234 JANE DOE ANYWHERE 234567 345 FLABBER GAST UNKNOWN 345678 Copy Note that the 3GL code treats every field that is not a STRING as an INTEGER. This happens to be sufficient for this particular entity. A more generic approach would probably use uprmgets and uprmputs to handle all fields not of type STRING. The 3GL code is as follows: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <ctype.h> void convert_customer () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hPrm; /* Parameter handle */ UHACT hEnt1, hEnt2; /* Entity handles */ long numOccs, numFlds; /* Nr of occurrrences and fields */ long iOcc, iFld; /* Indices of occurrence and field */ int fType; /* Field datatype */ UCHAR* strVal; /* Allocated string buffer */ UCHAR *p; /* Work pointer */ int intVal; /* Integer value */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC*/ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"CUSTSRV", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate operation GETCUST */ /* Instantiate operation SETCUST */ uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETCUST", 0, &hOp1); uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETCUST", 0, &hOp2); uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETCUST */ uprmgeth(hOp1, 1, 0, &hEnt1); /* Get OUT handle for CUSTOMER*/ uprmgeth(hOp2, 1, 0, &hEnt2); /* Get IN handle for CUSTOMER*/ uentoccs (hEnt1, &numOccs); /* Get number of occurrences */ uentflds (hEnt1, &numFlds); /* Get number of fields */ for ( iOcc=1; iOcc<=numOccs; iOcc++ )/* Loop through occurrences */ { uentseto(hEnt1, iOcc); /* Make occurrence current */ uentcreo(hEnt2, iOcc); /* Create new occ for SETCUST */ for ( iFld=1; iFld<=numFlds; iFld++ )/* Loop through fields */ { uprmgeth(hEnt1, iFld, 0, &hPrm); /* Get field handle */ uprmtype(hPrm, &fType); /* Get field type */ if ( fType == UTYPE_STRING ) /* Handle string field */ { uuf2str(hEnt1, iFld, &strVal); /* Get string data */ for ( p=strVal; *p; p++ ) *p = toupper(*p); /* Convert to uppercase */ ustr2uf(hEnt2, iFld, strVal); /* Put converted data */ unifree(hEnt1, strVal); /* Free string buffer */ } else { uuf2int(hEnt1, iFld, &intVal);/* Get integer value */ uint2uf(hEnt2, iFld, intVal); /* Put integer value */ } ufreeh(&hPrm); /* Free parameter handle */ } } uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation SETENT */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free GETCUST operation handle */ ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free SETCUST operation handle */ ufreeh (&hEnt1); /* Free entity handle */ ufreeh (&hEnt2); /* Free entity handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uentoccs Return the number of occurrences in the specified entity parameter. uprmputs Fill a basic parameter or entity field with Uniface-formatted data. uuf2int Copy an integer value from a basic parameter or entity field to an int program variable.
